text:
Searching for papers on neural coupling in music... 🔍

Here are **10 recent papers on neural coupling & music** from PubMed:

---

## 📚 Neural Coupling in Music

### 1. Functional Connectivity of Auditory, Motor, and Reward Networks at Rest and During Music Listening
- **Authors:** Han KYK, Wang J, Kubit BM, Parrish C, Loui P
- **Journal:** *Brain Sciences* (2025)
- **Affiliation:** Department of Psychology, Northeastern University, Boston, MA
- **DOI:** [10.3390/brainsci16010015](https://doi.org/10.3390/brainsci16010015)
- **TLDR:** Examines how auditory, motor, and reward networks interact during music listening vs. rest, arguing most studies look at these networks in isolation.

---

### 2. Nightingale: A Multimodal Approach Combining EEG Signals and Audio Features to Enhance Music Therapy
- **Authors:** Chen Z, Yadav N
- **Journal:** *PLoS ONE* (2026)
- **Affiliation:** The Webb Schools, Claremont, CA
- **DOI:** [10.1371/journal.pone.0341497](https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0341497)
- **TLDR:** Presents "Nightingale," integrating EEG + audio features to improve music therapy outcomes with a multimodal neural-audio coupling approach.

---

### 3. ⭐ Distinct Social Dynamics of Joint Action Represented by Interpersonal Neural Coupling in Joint Musical Performances
- **Authors:** Yuan D, Chan J, Shoaib Z, Chan KY, Kielman A, Wong PCM
- **Journal:** *NeuroImage* (2026)
- **Affiliation:** Brain and Mind Institute, Chinese University of Hong Kong
- **DOI:** [10.1016/j.neuroimage.2025.121662](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.neuroimage.2025.121662)
- **TLDR:** Uses hyperscanning to show that congruent vs. incongruent joint musical performance involves different interpersonal neural coupling dynamics — directly relevant to your speaker-listener coupling work! 🎯

---

### 4. Neural Mechanisms of Role Reversal in Improvisational Music Psychodrama: An fNIRS Hyperscanning Study
- **Authors:** Wang Y, Peng K, Zhang Y, Yao Y, Zhang Z, Zhao F, Zheng M
- **Journal:** *Brain Sciences* (2025)
- **Affiliation:** Department of Psychology, Southwest University, Chongqing
- **DOI:** [10.3390/brainsci15111235](https://doi.org/10.3390/brainsci15111235)
- **TLDR:** fNIRS hyperscanning study on interpersonal neural coupling during improvisational music psychodrama with role reversal conditions.

---

### 5. Impact of Neural Network-Quantified Musical Groove on Cyclists' Joint Coordination and Muscle Synergy
- **Authors:** Li H, Lin X, Wu X
- **Journal:** *J Neuroengineering and Rehabilitation* (2025)
- **Affiliation:** Shanghai University of Sport
- **DOI:** [10.1186/s12984-025-01778-7](https://doi.org/10.1186/s12984-025-01778-7)
- **TLDR:** Investigates how musical groove (rhythmic drive) influences auditory-motor coupling during cycling, quantified via neural networks.

---

### 6. Pleasant Odors Promote Soothing Autonomic Response and Brain-Body Coupling Through Respiratory Modulation
- **Authors:** Ghibaudo V, Turrel M, Granget J, Souilhol M, Garcia S, Plailly J, Buonviso N
- **Journal:** *Scientific Reports* (2025)
- **Affiliation:** Lyon Neuroscience Research Center (CRNL), Inserm, France
- **DOI:** [10.1038/s41598-025-20422-x](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41598-025-20422-x)
- **TLDR:** Compares how pleasant odors vs. pleasant music modulate brain-body coupling through respiratory rhythms. Music comparison included.

---

### 7. Creative Experiences and Brain Clocks
- **Authors:** Coronel-Oliveros C, Migeot J, Lehue F, ... Ibanez A (49 authors)
- **Journal:** *Nature Communications* (2025)
- **Affiliation:** Latin American Brain Health Institute (BrainLat), Universidad Adolfo Ibañez
- **DOI:** [10.1038/s41467-025-64173-9](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-025-64173-9)
- **TLDR:** Large-scale study showing creative experiences (including music) relate to "brain clocks" — deviations from chronological brain age, with implications for brain health.

---

## 🎯 Most Relevant to Your Research

Paper **#3 (Yuan et al., 2026)** is the strongest hit — interpersonal neural coupling during joint musical performance using hyperscanning. Directly connects to your speaker-listener neural coupling work with Dr. Abrams.

Paper **#4 (Wang et al.)** is also relevant — fNIRS hyperscanning during music interaction.

Want me to dig deeper into any of these, or refine the search (e.g., "interbrain synchrony music" or "EEG hyperscanning music")?
